assumptions: If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the counter value (Q) is set to 0.

cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.

stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed-cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
Q remains defined (no X/Z) after reset release.
Q changes according to the Johnson counter rule on successive clock edges.
cases.goal: Observe clock‑driven state progress after reset release and verify that Q evolves consistently.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
The enrichment adds explicit observation steps while preserving the original conservative intent.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.

expected_properties: When Q[0] is 0, the next Q has MSB set to 1 and the rest shifted right.
When Q[0] is 1, the next Q has MSB set to 0 and the rest shifted right.
Observed Q transitions follow the Johnson counter rule without X or Z values.
cases.expected_transition: Observe Q transition from a state with LSB=0 to the next state with MSB=1, then from LSB=1 to MSB=0.
cases.goal: Validate the direction‑change behavior of the Johnson counter when the least‑significant bit toggles.
notes: The case does not assume a fixed number of cycles for the transition; it observes the first two clock edges after reset release.
preconditions: Counter has been released from reset and observed for at least two clock cycles.
stimulus_intent: Release reset, allow the counter to run, capture Q before and after the LSB toggles.
timing_assumptions: Conservative observation across multiple clock cycles; no fixed latency is assumed.
